analysis.summary: Apache Superset is a web-based business intelligence platform that provides data visualization, exploration, and dashboarding capabilities. Built with a React TypeScript frontend and Python Flask backend, it processes SQL queries against various databases, transforms results into charts and dashboards, and manages user access through role-based permissions. The system follows a plugin-based architecture for extensible visualization types and database connectors.
capabilities: Executes SQL queries against 40+ database types including PostgreSQL, MySQL, BigQuery, Snowflake (superset/db_engine_specs/)
Renders 50+ chart types from bar charts to geospatial visualizations using React and D3.js (superset-frontend/plugins/)
Manages interactive dashboards with drag-and-drop layout, filtering, and cross-filtering (superset/dashboards/, superset-frontend/src/dashboard/)
Provides web-based SQL editor with syntax highlighting, query history, and result export (superset-frontend/src/SqlLab/)
Implements role-based access control with granular permissions on databases, datasets, charts, and dashboards (superset/security/)
Processes asynchronous queries using Celery for long-running database operations (superset/tasks/)
Caches query results using Redis or database backend to reduce load (superset/views/cache.py)
Generates alerts and reports on scheduled intervals via email or Slack (superset/reports/)
Supports embedding dashboards in external applications via iframe with guest tokens (superset-embedded-sdk/)
Manages semantic layer with virtual datasets, calculated columns, and custom metrics (superset/datasets/, superset/columns/)
Handles real-time dashboard updates through WebSocket connections (superset-websocket/)
Imports and exports dashboard configurations as JSON for version control (superset/importexport/)
Provides REST API for programmatic access to all platform features (docs/static/resources/openapi.json)
Processes Jinja2 templating in SQL queries for dynamic parameter substitution (superset/jinja_context.py)
Manages database connection pooling with health checks and failover (superset/databases/)
Implements native filters for dashboard-wide parameter controls (superset-frontend/src/dashboard/components/nativeFilters/)
Handles CSV/Excel file uploads to create temporary datasets (superset/datasets/commands/importers/)
Manages chart annotations with time-based overlays on visualizations (superset/annotation_layers/)
Processes geographical data for map visualizations with custom GeoJSON (superset-frontend/plugins/plugin-chart-country-map/)
Implements theme customization with CSS variables and brand colors (superset-frontend/src/assets/stylesheets/)
Manages internationalization with 20+ language translations (superset-frontend/src/locales/)
Handles data profiling and column metadata extraction from connected databases (superset/connectors/sqla/)
Processes advanced analytics functions like Prophet forecasting in time series charts (superset-frontend/plugins/plugin-chart-echarts/)
Manages plugin system for custom visualization types and database drivers (superset/extensions/)
Implements OAuth2, LDAP, and custom authentication providers (superset/security/manager.py)
use_cases: Data analysts create interactive dashboards to monitor KPIs and business metrics across multiple data sources
Business users explore data through no-code chart builder without writing SQL queries
Data engineers build and schedule automated reports for stakeholders via email or Slack
Developers embed analytics capabilities in their applications using the JavaScript SDK and REST API
Organizations implement self-service analytics with role-based access to sensitive data
analysis.target_audience: Data analysts, business users, data engineers, and developers building analytics applications
technical_stack: Flask 2.2+ for backend API framework (pyproject.toml dependencies)
SQLAlchemy 1.4+ for database ORM and query building (pyproject.toml)
Redis for caching and Celery task queue (docker-compose.yml)
Docker and Docker Compose for containerized deployment (docker-compose.yml)
api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ — List dashboards with pagination and filtering (docs/static/resources/openapi.json)
POST /api/v1/sqllab/execute/ — Execute SQL query and return results (docs/static/resources/openapi.json)
POST /api/v1/security/guest_token/ — Generate guest token for embedding (docs/static/resources/openapi.json)
GET /api/v1/chart/data — Execute chart query and return formatted data (docs/static/resources/openapi.json)
auth_methods: OAuth2 with Google, GitHub, Azure providers (superset/security/manager.py)
Database-based user management with bcrypt password hashing
JWT guest tokens for embedded analytics (superset/security/guest_token.py)
Role-based access control with granular permissions (superset/security/manager.py)
integrations: Redis for caching and task queue (docker-compose.yml, superset/cache.py)
Email SMTP for alerts and reports (superset/reports/notifications/email.py)
Celery with Redis/RabbitMQ for async processing (pyproject.toml)
S3 for file storage via boto3 (pyproject.toml dependencies)

architecture.pattern: monorepo with microservices - contains main Flask app, React frontend, WebSocket service, and embedded SDK
components: Flask backend API server handling authentication, queries, and metadata (superset/)
React TypeScript frontend for dashboard and chart interfaces (superset-frontend/)
WebSocket service for real-time dashboard updates (superset-websocket/)
Celery workers for async query processing and reporting (superset/tasks/)
Database engine abstraction layer supporting 40+ database types (superset/db_engine_specs/)
architecture.data_flow: User requests flow through Flask to database engines, query results are cached in Redis, transformed by chart plugins, and rendered in React. WebSocket service pushes real-time updates. Async operations use Celery with Redis queue.
architecture.persistence: PostgreSQL/MySQL for application metadata, Redis for caching and sessions, user databases for actual data querying. SQLAlchemy ORM with Alembic migrations.
architecture.concurrency_model: async/await with Flask-CORS for API, React with Redux for state management, Celery workers for background processing
architecture.communication_patterns: HTTP REST API between frontend/backend, WebSocket for real-time updates, Redis pub/sub for distributed caching, Celery message queue for async tasks
architecture.plugin_extension_model: Python entry points for database drivers and backend extensions, React dynamic imports for chart plugins, configuration-driven feature flags

primary_entities: Dashboard - container for charts with layout and filters (superset/dashboards/models.py)
Chart/Slice - individual visualization with query and formatting (superset/charts/models.py)
Dataset - logical table with columns, metrics, and permissions (superset/datasets/models.py)
Database - connection configuration with credentials and settings (superset/databases/models.py)
Query - SQL execution history and caching (superset/models/sql_lab.py)
storage_backends: PostgreSQL/MySQL for application metadata and configuration
Redis for query result caching and session storage
User-configured databases for actual data queries
File system for uploaded CSVs and assets
data_model.schema_patterns: Relational model with SQLAlchemy ORM, foreign key relationships, JSON fields for flexible configuration
data_model.migration_strategy: Alembic database migrations in superset/migrations/ with versioned schema changes

algorithms.problem_type: Interactive business intelligence with real-time querying, caching, and visualization
core_patterns: Repository pattern for data access abstraction (superset/daos/)
Factory pattern for database engine creation (superset/db_engine_specs/)
Observer pattern for real-time dashboard updates (superset-websocket/)
Decorator pattern for security and caching (superset/security/)
algorithms.complexity_indicators: Handles large datasets with pagination, implements query result caching, manages concurrent users with session isolation
key_algorithms: Query result caching with TTL and invalidation (superset/cache.py)
Role-based permission checking with hierarchical inheritance (superset/security/)
Dashboard filter propagation across multiple charts (superset-frontend/src/dashboard/)
Time series forecasting with Prophet integration (superset-frontend/plugins/)
algorithms.evidence: superset/cache.py implements LRU caching, superset/security/manager.py contains RBAC logic, superset-frontend/src/dashboard/components/nativeFilters/ handles filter propagation

security_model.authentication: Flask-Login sessions, OAuth2 providers, LDAP/Active Directory, database users with bcrypt hashing
security_model.authorization: Role-based access control with granular permissions on databases, datasets, charts, dashboards. Row-level security via SQL WHERE clauses
security_model.data_protection: HTTPS enforcement, SQL injection prevention via SQLAlchemy, XSS protection, CSRF tokens, guest token encryption for embedding
security_model.security_headers: CORS configuration, Content Security Policy, secure cookie settings in Flask configuration
security_model.vulnerability_handling: SECURITY.md file present, dependency scanning, regular security updates documented in release notes

infrastructure: PostgreSQL or MySQL for metadata storage
Redis for caching and Celery message broker

deployment.container_orchestration: Docker Compose with separate containers for web, worker, Redis, PostgreSQL (docker-compose.yml)
deployment.ci_cd: GitHub Actions with Python unittest workflow (README.md references)
deployment.environment_config: Environment variables for database URLs, Redis connection, feature flags. Docker environment files and Python configuration modules (docker/pythonpath_dev/superset_config.py)
deployment.scaling_model: Horizontal scaling via multiple web server and Celery worker containers, Redis clustering for cache, database read replicas

code_quality_signals.test_framework: pytest for Python backend, Jest/Cypress for frontend testing
code_quality_signals.test_count_estimate: 1308 test files found across Python and TypeScript
code_quality_signals.documentation_quality: well-documented with comprehensive user/admin/developer guides, OpenAPI spec, architectural documentation
code_quality_signals.code_style: Black for Python formatting, ESLint/Prettier for TypeScript, pre-commit hooks configured

project_health.recent_activity: Recent commit shows active bug fixes in datasource access validation
project_health.community_signals: Code of conduct present, security policy documented, issue templates, comprehensive release notes, Apache Software Foundation governance

files_analyzed: pyproject.toml - Python dependencies and build configuration
information_gaps: Exact performance benchmarks and scaling limits
Complete list of supported database driver versions
Specific enterprise features and licensing model

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: System provides extensible plugin architecture for visualization types (50+ chart plugins) and database connectors (40+ engine specs), with dynamic loading and configuration-driven feature activation. Core platform orchestrates plugin execution while maintaining consistent interfaces.
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: Evidence: REST API with OpenAPI spec provides structured contracts, SQLAlchemy models enforce relational schemas, but supports flexible JSON configuration fields and schema-less query results from diverse databases
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: Evidence from superset/daos/ repository pattern, superset/databases/models.py ORM operations, query result caching in superset/cache.py, and standard database CRUD through SQLAlchemy with role-based filtering
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: Evidence from Redis caching layer, Celery async task processing, WebSocket real-time updates, and multi-user dashboard sharing with potential cache staleness tolerance
functional_dna.abstract_problem: Transforms structured queries into visual representations through a plugin ecosystem, managing user permissions and caching intermediate results while orchestrating real-time collaborative access to multiple data sources.
functional_dna.data_flow_pattern: Request → Authentication → Query Execution → Result Caching → Plugin Transformation → Visual Rendering, with WebSocket broadcasts for real-time updates and async task processing for heavy operations
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: Core patterns of plugin orchestration, query abstraction, caching, and role-based access are highly reusable. The React frontend architecture and Flask API patterns translate well to other domains. However, visualization-specific plugins and SQL query optimization are domain-locked. The authentication, caching, and plugin loading mechanisms could power content management systems, API gateways, or any multi-tenant platform requiring extensible data transformation.

evidence_claims.claim: Supports 40+ database types through plugin architecture
Implements role-based access control with granular permissions
Uses Redis for query result caching and session storage
evidence_claims.evidence: docker-compose.yml shows Redis service, superset/cache.py implements caching layer

evidence_claims.claim: Provides REST API with OpenAPI specification
Supports real-time dashboard updates via WebSocket
evidence_claims.evidence: superset-websocket/ directory contains WebSocket service implementation

cross_industry_applications: Content Management Systems - plugin-based content transformation with role-based publishing
IoT Device Management - sensor data visualization with real-time dashboards and device-specific plugins
Financial Trading Platforms - multi-source data aggregation with permission-controlled access and real-time updates
Educational Assessment Systems - test result visualization with institution-specific plugins and student privacy controls
Manufacturing Quality Control - production metrics dashboards with equipment-specific data connectors
Healthcare Analytics - patient data visualization with HIPAA-compliant access controls and medical device integrations
E-commerce Analytics - sales performance dashboards with marketplace-specific plugins and merchant isolation
Social Media Management - multi-platform content analytics with brand-specific visualization themes

complexity_factors: Complex plugin loading and configuration system
Database abstraction layer supporting multiple engines
friction_report.migration_path: Extract core plugin orchestration framework, adapt authentication layer, replace visualization plugins with domain-specific transformers, maintain caching and real-time update mechanisms

output.delivery: sync and async with WebSocket streaming

authentication.method: Flask-Login sessions, OAuth2, JWT tokens, RBAC permissions

comparable_systems: Grafana - plugin-based monitoring dashboards with multi-source data integration
WordPress - plugin ecosystem for content management with role-based access
Jenkins - plugin architecture for CI/CD with job orchestration and user permissions
Jupyter Hub - multi-user notebook environment with kernel plugins and resource management
Kibana - Elasticsearch visualization platform with dashboard sharing and real-time updates
information_gaps: Exact performance benchmarks for concurrent user loads
Complete list of supported database driver versions and compatibility matrix
Enterprise licensing model and advanced security features
Detailed plugin development SDK documentation and certification process
